title: A notebook I started in Quito and finished in Manila
summary: A short reflection about a notebook that crossed countries and held different parts of your life.
topic: A notebook I started in Quito and finished in Manila
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a notebook, sketchbook, or journal that travelled with you across different places or seasons of your life. Describe the first page, the last page, and what changed. Use past simple and present perfect.
hintStarters: I bought the small black notebook on a rainy Tuesday in…
By the time I wrote the last page, I was sitting in…
sampleText: I bought the small black notebook on a rainy Tuesday in Quito for the equivalent of two dollars. The cover was already a little soft, as if the shop had kept it on the shelf for years. The first page was about the soup I had eaten for lunch — locro de papa — and the smell of wet eucalyptus on the way back to my hostel. My handwriting at that time was big and slow, because I was still learning to slow down. Over the next eleven months, the notebook travelled with me through Bogotá, then by plane to Manila, and finally into a small apartment near a Jeepney terminal. The ink colour changed three times. My handwriting became smaller and quicker. The last page, written one humid evening in May, was about a long conversation with my new flatmate about why she had moved home. She cried a little. I did too. When I closed the notebook for the last time, I noticed that the cover was now soft like skin. I have kept it in a drawer ever since, and I have opened it only twice. Some books are written to be read again, and some are written so we know who we were that year.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for actions before another past action
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'as if the shop had kept it on the shelf for years' và 'the soup I had eaten for lunch' — past perfect chỉ hành động đã hoàn thành trước thời điểm nói đến trong câu chính.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'As if the shop had kept it on the shelf for years' and 'the soup I had eaten for lunch' — past perfect for actions completed before the moment referenced in the main clause.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect + 'ever since'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I have kept it in a drawer ever since' dùng present perfect để chỉ một trạng thái kéo dài từ thời điểm quá khứ ('ever since' = từ đó đến nay) tới hiện tại.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I have kept it in a drawer ever since' uses present perfect for a state stretching from a past moment ('ever since' = from then) to the present.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi mua cuốn sổ đen nhỏ vào một thứ Ba mưa ở Quito với giá tương đương hai đô la. Bìa đã hơi mềm, như thể cửa hàng đã giữ nó trên kệ nhiều năm. Trang đầu tiên viết về món súp tôi đã ăn vào bữa trưa — locro de papa — và mùi bạch đàn ướt trên đường về nhà trọ. Chữ viết của tôi lúc đó to và chậm, vì tôi vẫn đang học cách chậm lại. Trong mười một tháng tiếp theo, cuốn sổ đi cùng tôi qua Bogotá, sau đó bằng máy bay đến Manila, và cuối cùng vào một căn hộ nhỏ gần một bến xe Jeepney. Màu mực thay đổi ba lần. Chữ viết của tôi trở nên nhỏ hơn và nhanh hơn. Trang cuối, viết vào một buổi tối tháng Năm ẩm ướt, kể về một cuộc trò chuyện dài với người bạn cùng phòng mới về lý do cô ấy đã trở về nhà. Cô ấy khóc một chút. Tôi cũng vậy. Khi tôi gấp cuốn sổ lần cuối, tôi nhận ra rằng bìa giờ đã mềm như da. Tôi đã giữ nó trong ngăn kéo kể từ đó, và tôi đã mở nó chỉ hai lần. Có những cuốn sách được viết để đọc lại, và có những cuốn được viết để chúng ta biết mình là ai năm đó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sentence about the purchase.
options: I bought the small black notebook on a rainy Tuesday in Quito.
I buyed the small black notebook on a rainy Tuesday in Quito.
I have bought the small black notebook on a rainy Tuesday in Quito.
I am buying the small black notebook on a rainy Tuesday in Quito.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past perfect sentence with 'as if'.
options: as if the shop had kept it on the shelf for years
as if the shop has kept it on the shelf for years
as if the shop was kept it on the shelf for years
as if the shop had keep it on the shelf for years
mcQuestions.explanation: 'As if + past perfect' for imagined prior situation.

options: the way a person writes by hand
the speed of typing
the kind of paper used
the colour of ink
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Handwriting' = the personal style of writing by hand.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sentence about travel.
options: The notebook travelled with me through Bogotá and finally into a small apartment.
The notebook travel with me through Bogotá and finally into a small apartment.
The notebook is travelling with me through Bogotá and finally into a small apartment.
The notebook have travelled with me through Bogotá and finally into a small apartment.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'travelled' for the journey of the object across past time.

options: My handwriting became smaller and quicker.
My handwriting became more small and more quick.
My handwriting becomes smaller and quicker.
My handwriting became smallest and quickest.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present perfect sentence.
options: I have kept it in a drawer ever since.
I have kept it in a drawer since ever.
I am kept it in a drawer ever since.
I had kept it in a drawer ever since.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect 'have kept' + 'ever since' for unbroken span from past to present.

options: the coloured liquid used by a pen to write
the cover of a book
a kind of paper
a kind of small bird

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about quantity of times.
options: I have opened it only twice.
I have opened it only two time.
I am opened it only twice.
I opened it only twice already.
the cover was now soft like skin
the cover was now soft as skin only
the cover was now soft like the skin one
the cover was now softer like skin
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Soft like + noun' is a natural simile pattern in A2 register.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past perfect sentence inside reported reasoning.
options: a long conversation with my new flatmate about why she had moved home
a long conversation with my new flatmate about why she has moved home
a long conversation with my new flatmate about why she did moved home
a long conversation with my new flatmate about why she moves home
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had moved' for an earlier past action referenced in a past-tense conversation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural closing line.
options: Some books are written to be read again, and some are written so we know who we were that year.
Some books are written to be read again, and some are written so we know who we are that year.
Some books are wrote to be read again, and some are wrote so we know who we were that year.
Some books are written to be read again, and some are write so we know who we were that year.
mcQuestions.explanation: Passive 'are written' + infinitive 'to be read'; past 'were' for past identity.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do some notebooks fill up faster in some seasons of life than others? What does the speed of a notebook tell you about who you were at that time?
